sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Terminology 38:1

items.sefariaRef: Talmud Eser HaSefirot, Section V, List of Answers on Terminology 38:1
items.html: The light of Hesed in the vessel of Bina is considered a “thin and weak light.” This is because it is from the coupling of coarseness of phase one, in which there is no illumination of GAR, which is the self and sustenance of any light. Hence, it must remain in the vessel of Bina as long as it does not obtain illumination of Hochma, so as to suckle GAR from the vessel of Bina. This is why it is called “needing its mother,” meaning Bina. When it obtains illumination of Hochma, it is considered to have obtained its Gadlut [greatness/adulthood], and no longer needs its mother Bina. (Item 39, and Inner Light, Sub Header, “The seven sons”)
